summary: Define stable IDs, Definition/Runtime State separation, and WorldState v0
acceptance_criteria: M1 entity references use stable branded primitive IDs; raw numbers/strings cannot cross into authoritative state without parser/schema validation.
Immutable Definition records are separate from mutable runtime State tables; runtime rules do not look up core entities by display name.
WorldState v0 contains only M1 kernel fields: meta, current GameDay, revision, seed/content manifest references, minimal entity/graph tables, scheduler hooks, and state hash inputs.
WorldState v0 avoids cyclic object graphs and object-identity serialization; relationships use IDs or explicit edge tables.
A canonical deterministic state hash is defined over WorldState v0 with stable field order, stable entity order, and documented compatibility expectations.
Basic semantic invariants exist for duplicate IDs, bad references, missing definitions, invalid current day/revision, and hash stability.

No full economy, population, vassal politics, diplomacy, war, siege, combat, map geometry, historical database, Rust/WASM, server, runtime SQL database, arbitrary-code mod support, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Unit and property tests cover branded ID construction, schema rejection of invalid IDs, Definition/State separation, duplicate/bad references, and canonical hash stability.
Architecture/static guard proves packages/sim-core imports no React, PixiJS, Electron, DOM, Node fs/path IO, real-time authority API, or render-frame API.
notes: M0 gate is dependency-satisfied by FOUNDATION-001 through FOUNDATION-008 all being CLOSED.
If implementation requires changing accepted ADR semantics or frozen PROJECT_STATUS decisions, stop and route to systems_architect before writing code.
